{"key_event":"Financial collapse and post-2019 state breakdown","reasoning":"Lebanon’s state power fell sharply as default, currency collapse, service failure, institutional vacancies, and militia-led security decisions left the formal state with very weak administrative, fiscal, and coercive capacity, only partly offset by the 2025 presidency and possible army role. Civil society retained unusually high mobilizational capacity through the Thawra, independent media, victims’ groups, NGOs, diaspora support, and Change MPs, but economic ruin, emigration, sectarian fragmentation, repression/impunity, and war displacement materially reduced its autonomy and effectiveness.","society_change":-0.7,"society_power":6.2,"state_change":-1.2,"state_power":3.0}
